packets.title: Morning dispatch and blocked-work redispatch
packets.purpose: Specify a buildable supervisory dispatch surface that recovers partially completed vineyard work through canonical assignment, acknowledgement, execution, completion, and verification states without erasing operational history.
preconditions: The work order resolves to one stable block identity
The plan is approved before assignment
The foreman acknowledges current instructions before execution
Every state change has an actor and timestamp
Target and actual acreage use the same unit

state_contract.history_policy: Append transitions; never rewrite partial, blocked, redispatch, acknowledgement, correction, or prior completion events after later verification.

may_not: Approve work
Verify final closure
Backdate another person's field report
Erase a partial or blocker event
Verify final closure
Rewrite completed acreage
Verify final closure
Execute before acknowledgement
Erase a blocker event

may: Provide field evidence to the foreman
may_not: Redispatch blocked work
Verify final closure

interaction_contract.precondition: Current state is in_progress and reported acreage is valid
interaction_contract.result: State becomes partially_completed
interaction_contract.audit_event: Append partial completion with actor, local time, acreage, and reason

interaction_contract.precondition: Repair evidence exists and current state is blocked
interaction_contract.result: State becomes assigned for the remaining scope
interaction_contract.audit_event: Append coordinator redispatch without changing the blocker or partial record

interaction_contract.precondition: Current state is assigned and field instructions are current
interaction_contract.result: State becomes acknowledged
interaction_contract.audit_event: Append foreman acknowledgement with actor and time

interaction_contract.precondition: Current state is acknowledged and the repaired equipment is available
interaction_contract.result: State becomes in_progress
interaction_contract.audit_event: Append resumed execution with actor, time, and remaining row scope

interaction_contract.precondition: Current state is in_progress and final actual acreage is valid
interaction_contract.result: State becomes completed
interaction_contract.audit_event: Append final scope and actual-use report without rewriting the earlier partial

interaction_contract.precondition: Current state is completed and actual acres equal target acres
interaction_contract.result: State becomes verified
interaction_contract.audit_event: Append supervisor verification with actor and time

interaction_contract.precondition: User can view the work order
interaction_contract.result: Immutable ordered history is visible
interaction_contract.audit_event: No workflow state mutation

error_messages: Repair evidence is required before redispatching the remaining rows.
The current assignment must be acknowledged before work resumes.
Completed acreage cannot exceed the block acreage.
The work order changed elsewhere; review the newer status before retrying.

acceptance_criteria: Every FIX-001 status edge resolves to a transition in canonical WF-001.
The screen exposes partial acreage, blocked reason, affected rows, reporter, and time without opening a secondary record.
Recovery follows blocked to assigned to acknowledged to in_progress before completion.
Partial acreage survives redispatch and final completion as an immutable historical event.
Only a vineyard manager can transition completed work to verified.
The rendered slice resolves WF-001, SCN-001-01, FIX-001, SCR-001, CMP-001, and PKT-001.
Status meaning remains understandable without color and at a 375 CSS-pixel viewport.

test_cases.assertion: Approved work is assigned, acknowledged, and started through canonical transitions.
A partial event preserves 11.2 completed acres before the remaining-scope assignment is blocked.
Repair evidence redispatches only the remaining rows through assignment and acknowledgement while retaining prior history.
A foreman cannot perform the manager-only verification transition.
Every operational state has a visible text label and non-color cue.
Final actual acreage equals the 18.6-acre target and never exceeds block acreage.
An offline partial event remains an immutable note when a newer assigned server state wins conflict resolution.

packets.title: Seasonal plan, dispatch, field execution, and verification
packets.purpose: Specify the complete responsive and field execution package from a daily operational brief through approved seasonal work, dispatch, acknowledgement, completion, correction, verification, and immutable history.
preconditions: Every operational record resolves through stable organization, property, block, and person identifiers.
Consequential actions use current default-deny permission, transition, notification, and sync contracts.
Accepted history is append-only; corrections and supersessions retain reciprocal predecessor and successor lineage.
Fixture, scenario, state, actor, record, and event identifiers remain visible at review boundaries.

state_contract.history_policy: Append attributable events and reciprocal correction or supersession lineage; never overwrite an accepted observation, assignment, execution, completion, time, payroll, or allocation fact.

may_not: Exercise an action without an explicit permission rule and matching scope
Erase accepted history or act as another person

Erase accepted history or act as another person

error_messages: This action is unavailable because current authority or scope does not match the record.
The record changed elsewhere; review the newer event before retrying.
Required evidence is incomplete. Accepted facts remain unchanged.
Offline work is queued with its original actor and capture time; no server fact was overwritten.

dependencies: Connected synthetic operation, events, records, and scenario fixtures under data/
Canopy Signal DTCG tokens and generated CSS
Default-deny permission, notification, and synchronization registries
Wave-001 screen, component, capture, review, and Figma manifests
acceptance_criteria: Every SCR-001, SCR-002, SCR-003, SCR-004, SCR-005, SCR-006 route resolves its canonical scenario, fixture, state, action, permission, transition, event, and record identifiers.
Normal, urgent, blocked, partial, corrected, historical, offline, stale, conflict, loading, empty, error, and completion states appear where allocated by the wave contract.
Every consequential action is default-deny, names its disabled reason, and appends rather than rewrites accepted history.
Desktop and field surfaces preserve the same stable identity, state, accountable owner, effective time, and lineage.
Keyboard, focus, target-size, live-region, non-color, reduced-motion, overflow, print, and axe checks pass for the packet scope.
Direct screen-to-packet trace edges resolve every PKT-002 screen without relying on an unrelated component edge.

test_cases.assertion: The primary fixture advances only through canonical transitions with exact actor and event attribution.
Blocked or urgent work preserves accepted evidence, affected scope, owner, clock, and bounded recovery.
Recovery appends a new event and retains the original exception, partial fact, or predecessor record.
An actor without an explicit matching permission rule sees a disabled action and cannot mutate state.
State, urgency, disabled reason, progress, and lineage remain understandable without color or pointer input.
Canonical desktop, mobile, 200-percent-equivalent reflow, and print targets preserve operational meaning without page overflow.
Rendered values resolve to connected fixture events and records rather than disconnected sample copy.

figma_mapping.frame_name: PKT-002 · Seasonal plan, dispatch, field execution, and verification

interaction_contract.precondition: The actor satisfies PERM-038 and the current state is payroll_accepted.
interaction_contract.result: The workflow advances through TRN-WF-007-007 to cost_allocated.
interaction_contract.audit_event: Append workflow.reconciliation with actor, assignment, timestamp, reason policy, record snapshot, and idempotency key.

interaction_contract.precondition: The actor can view the exact record and organization scope.
interaction_contract.result: The attributable current record and immutable history are visible.
interaction_contract.audit_event: No workflow state mutation; preserve the inspected record identity.

error_messages: This action is unavailable because current authority or scope does not match the record.
The record changed elsewhere; review the newer event before retrying.
Required evidence is incomplete. Accepted facts remain unchanged.
Offline work is queued with its original actor and capture time; no server fact was overwritten.

dependencies: Connected synthetic operation, events, records, and scenario fixtures under data/
Canopy Signal DTCG tokens and generated CSS
Default-deny permission, notification, and synchronization registries
Wave-001 screen, component, capture, review, and Figma manifests
acceptance_criteria: Every SCR-043, SCR-044, SCR-045 route resolves its canonical scenario, fixture, state, action, permission, transition, event, and record identifiers.
Normal, urgent, blocked, partial, corrected, historical, offline, stale, conflict, loading, empty, error, and completion states appear where allocated by the wave contract.
Every consequential action is default-deny, names its disabled reason, and appends rather than rewrites accepted history.
Desktop and field surfaces preserve the same stable identity, state, accountable owner, effective time, and lineage.
Keyboard, focus, target-size, live-region, non-color, reduced-motion, overflow, print, and axe checks pass for the packet scope.
Direct screen-to-packet trace edges resolve every PKT-004 screen without relying on an unrelated component edge.

test_cases.assertion: The primary fixture advances only through canonical transitions with exact actor and event attribution.
Blocked or urgent work preserves accepted evidence, affected scope, owner, clock, and bounded recovery.
Recovery appends a new event and retains the original exception, partial fact, or predecessor record.
An actor without an explicit matching permission rule sees a disabled action and cannot mutate state.
State, urgency, disabled reason, progress, and lineage remain understandable without color or pointer input.
Canonical desktop, mobile, 200-percent-equivalent reflow, and print targets preserve operational meaning without page overflow.
Rendered values resolve to connected fixture events and records rather than disconnected sample copy.

figma_mapping.frame_name: PKT-004 · Time review, payroll exception, and cost allocation
